items.body:
## Summary

As of 2026-03-26, commit [`7066e51`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/7066e519725353d1abf38d9ca591ecd912fce1ba) (HEAD of `main`), CI reports failures across 4 service directories with actual test failures and 2 additional directories with CI-infrastructure-level failures (0 failed tests). All test failures occur exclusively in **live/AAD test mode** — none are reproducible locally without Azure credentials. The communication-identity and communication-short-codes failures are marked **recurring** (pre-existing), while the attestation failures are **new** (22 new failures).

---

## Failures

### `@azure/attestation`

**Failing tests (22 new failures, check run [68686295192](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68686295192)):**
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestOpenEnclaveShared`
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestOpenEnclaveAad`
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestOpenEnclaveIsolated`
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #AttestSgxEnclaveShared`
- *(and ~18 additional AAD-mode attestation tests)*

**Stats:** 22 failed / 190 passed / 212 total (10.38% failure rate)

**Error:**
```
AttestationToken is not yet valid.
```

**Root cause:**
The live attestation service returns JWT tokens with an `nbf` (not-before) claim that is more than 10 seconds ahead of the CI runner's clock. In `sdk/attestation/attestation/test/utils/recordedClient.ts` the client is configured with:
```ts
validateNotBeforeTime: !isPlaybackMode(),
timeValidationSlack: 10, // 10 seconds slack in validation time.
```
And in `src/models/attestationToken.ts` the check is:
```ts
if (nbfTime > timeNow) {
  const delta = nbfTime - timeNow;
  if (delta > (options.timeValidationSlack ?? 0)) {
    problems.push("AttestationToken is not yet valid.");
  }
}
```
The 10-second `timeValidationSlack` is no longer sufficient — the clock skew between CI runners and the attestation service now exceeds this threshold.

**Suggested fix:**
Increase `timeValidationSlack` in `recordedClient.ts` to a larger value (e.g., 30–60 seconds), or widen the slack only when running in CI:

````diff
--- a/sdk/attestation/attestation/test/utils/recordedClient.ts
+++ b/sdk/attestation/attestation/test/utils/recordedClient.ts
@@ ... @@
         validateNotBeforeTime: !isPlaybackMode(),
-        timeValidationSlack: 10, // 10 seconds slack in validation time.
+        timeValidationSlack: 60, // 60 seconds slack to handle CI clock skew.
```
(The same change appears in two places in `recordedClient.ts`, at approximately lines 78–80 and 112–113.)

**Reproduced locally:** No (live AAD test, requires Azure credentials)

---

### `@azure/communication-identity`

**Failing tests (12 recurring failures, check run [68682302045](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68682302045)):**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] > *` (multiple parameterized variants)
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD] > *` (multiple parameterized variants)

**Test file:** `sdk/communication/communication-identity/test/public/node/getTokenForTeamsUser.node.spec.ts`

**Stats:** 12 failed / 509 passed / 641 total (1.87% failure rate, all recurring)

**Error:**
```
invalid_grant: Error(s): 5000224 - Timestamp: 2026-03-26 09:14:47Z
Description: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: 97f8975a-d085-407d-aa3d-8dff81751700
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure AD tenant/resource used for `Get Token For Teams User` live tests returns `AADSTS5000224`, indicating the targeted resource (Microsoft Teams user AAD resource in the test tenant) is unavailable or decommissioned. These tests are marked **recurring** — they have been failing consistently across multiple CI runs. This is a live service/tenant configuration issue, not a code bug.

**Suggested fix:**
This is an infrastructure issue. The test tenant needs to have the Teams user resource properly provisioned, or the live test environment needs to be refreshed. As a short-term workaround, the tests could be skipped via the existing `SKIP_INT_IDENTITY_EXCHANGE_TOKEN_TEST=true` environment variable until the tenant is fixed.

**Reproduced locally:** No (live AAD test, requires Azure credentials and tenant configuration)

---

### `@azure/communication-short-codes`

**Failing tests (7 recurring failures, check run [68680458910](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68680458910)):**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments` (multiple platform variants)

**Stats:** 7 failed / 134 passed / 142 total (4.93% failure rate, all recurring)

**Error:**
```
The server encountered an internal error.
```

**Root cause:**
The Short Codes service is returning HTTP 500 internal server errors for the `manage Attachments` live test scenario. These are **recurring** failures — a persistent service-side issue, not a code bug. The live test environment may have stale or invalid attachment resources.

**Suggested fix:**
This is a service-side infrastructure issue. The live test resources (short code attachments) may need to be refreshed or the test environment re-provisioned. No code change is needed.

**Reproduced locally:** No (live service test, requires Azure resources)

---

### `@azure/digital-twins-core`

**Failing tests:** 0 (518 passed / 7 skipped / 525 total)

**Check run:** [68683989356](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68683989356) — marked as failed despite 0 test failures.

**Error:**
```
Error while trying to get OIDC token: Error: Request timeout: /azure-sdk/_apis/distributedtask
Error while trying to get OIDC token: Error: connect ETIMEDOUT 150.171.73.16:443
````

**Root cause:**
The CI pipeline failed to acquire an OIDC token from Azure DevOps due to a network timeout. This is a transient CI infrastructure issue unrelated to any code change. All 518 tests actually passed.

**Suggested fix:**
Re-run the CI pipeline. No code change is needed.

**Reproduced locally:** No (CI infrastructure issue)

---

## CI Infrastructure Failures (0 test failures)

Two additional check runs were marked `failure` but had **zero failing tests**:

| Package | Check Run | Actual Test Results | Root Cause |
|---|---|---|---|
| `@azure/ai-document-intelligence` (documentintelligence) | [68672895961](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68672895961) | 135 passed, 0 failed | Python `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable` in CI pipeline setup script |
| `@azure/core-*` (core) | [68672400574](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/68672400574) | 6,908 passed, 0 failed, 73 skipped | `Cmd.exe exited with code '1'` in CI browser test orchestration (also Python traceback in `Build Analyze` sub-job) |

These are CI pipeline configuration or runner issues, not test failures. No code change is needed.

---

## Additional Notes

- **attestation** is the only package with **new** failures (22 new); all others with test failures are **recurring**.
- All test failures occur exclusively in **live/AAD mode**. Playback-mode tests are unaffected.
- The attestation `timeValidationSlack` fix is the only code change that could directly address one of these failures.
- Local reproduction was not possible in this environment due to the private Azure DevOps npm registry (`pkgs.dev.azure.com`) requiring authentication that is not available in the CI analysis environment.
- CI run date: 2026-03-26, Build `#20260326.1`.
items.title: CI test failures on main: attestation, communication-identity, communication-short-codes, digital-twins-core
